title: Odontophyllum runcinatum
intro: Odontophyllum runcinatum, also known as Odontonema runcinatum, is a flowering plant species belonging to the family Acanthaceae. Traditionally, Odontophyllum runcinatum has been used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive problems, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Odontophyllum runcinatum include flavonoids, terpenoids, and phenolic compounds, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Odontophyllum runcinatum include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat fever, cough, and respiratory issues.
taxonomy: Odontophyllum runcinatum is classified within the family Orchidaceae, commonly known as the orchid family. It is a species of orchid that belongs to the subtribe Coelogyninae.
